brief_summary: Switch cross-section diagram for the H2 'The housing, in two halves'. Top housing drawn with thicker stroke and shine-hatching to suggest PC clarity. Bottom housing with damp hatching wash to suggest nylon absorption. POM stem in the middle. Three margin callouts: left-top 'PC top — clarity / top-end / harder, lube stays on rails'; right-middle 'POM stem — low friction / long rail dwell'; left-bottom 'nylon bottom — thock / low-mid lossy / tick to pock conversion'. A coral dashed seam line marks the resin transition with the italic caption 'the seam'.
data_sources: gateron-oil-king-deep-dive.mdx § 'The housing, in two halves' — 'polycarbonate top, nylon bottom, and POM stem ... Polycarbonate is the brightest of the three resins ... nylon is dense, absorbent, and acoustically lossy in the low-mid range ... POM is a self-lubricating thermoplastic ... The genuine engineering decision is in the seam between the two halves.'
